reasoning: The resolution criteria require 10 times the baseline 30-day average of ~531 protests, equating to roughly 5,320 protests in a single 30-day period. This necessitates an average of ~177 protests every single day for a month. Historically, major nationwide action days in France—such as those during the 2023 pension reforms or recent October 2025 austerity protests—generate demonstrations in roughly 200 to 250 towns on that specific day. To reach 5,320 ACLED-recorded events, France would need to sustain that single-day maximum capability almost every day for 30 consecutive days. Even massive sustained movements like the Yellow Vests operated primarily on weekly (Saturday) cycles, falling mathematically far short of this threshold. For the prompt's nearest date (Nov 2), we already know from recent news that no such daily mobilization has occurred over the past month. Therefore, the likelihood of clearing this exceptionally high standard is near zero. Probabilities are set at the 0.05 system floor and scale up slightly over longer horizons to reflect standard uncertainty.
